mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound, the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows is structurally compressed, producing a persistent tailwind for S&P 500 forward returns over the next month regardless of short-term news flow.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are not significantly higher than unconditional baseline when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 8%, markets price in aggressive forthcoming policy support and base-effect labor recovery, causing forward-looking equity rallies over a quarterly horizon as investors anticipate the trough in unemployment.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns following UNRATE>8% triggers are negative or indistinguishable from zero in more than 50% of historical episodes, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive month-over-month growth in PAYEMS and INDPRO signals a broad-based synchronized recovery across labor and production, a joint condition that predicts stronger S&P 500 gains over the next month than either series alone would imply.
failure_condition: Joint-positive PAYEMS/INDPRO episodes produce 20d forward returns no better than the unconditional mean (t-test p>0.10), or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC versus single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery regime, a rebound in RSAFS (retail sales) signals resurgent consumer demand that feeds directly into near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns show no significant positive correlation with RSAFS MoM growth (Rank IC < 0.02) across rolling 1-year windows during recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in HOUST exceeding 10% signals deteriorating forward housing demand and household balance-sheet stress, which historically leads equity markets lower over a quarterly horizon as the drag propagates to broader consumption.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns after HOUST YoY<-10% triggers are positive in more than 55% of episodes, or Rank IC magnitude < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial capacity utilization and corporate earnings power, which predicts a modest continuation of S&P 500 gains over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO MoM growth and 20d forward returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains low while FEDFUNDS stays near zero, real policy rates are deeply negative, which mechanically boosts equity valuations via reduced discount rates; this joint condition produces stronger quarterly S&P 500 gains than either series predicts individually.
failure_condition: Episodes with FEDFUNDS<0.5% and CPIAUCSL YoY<3% show 60d forward returns not significantly different from baseline, or the interaction model's Rank IC does not exceed the best single-series model by more than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: In a deep recession regime where UNRATE exceeds 9% concurrently with a three-month decline in PAYEMS, labor market deterioration signals ongoing demand destruction that outweighs recovery hopes, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns following joint UNRATE>9% and PAYEMS decline triggers are positive in more than 55% of episodes, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows within crisis regimes.
